channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Hrishikesh Hirway writes songs to keep his mother's memory alive
item.description: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Musician and podcast host Hrishikesh Hirway talks about paying attention to the miracle of being alive.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, famous guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Musician and podcast host Hrishikesh Hirway talks about paying attention to the miracle of being alive.

item.title: How lost-and-found networks reconnect personal belongings to disaster survivors
item.description: Extreme weather often washes away personal belongings. Across the country, lost-and-found networks seek to reunite these objects with disaster survivors.
item.content:encoded: Extreme weather often washes away personal belongings. Across the country, lost-and-found networks seek to reunite these objects with disaster survivors.
item.dc:creator: Laura Hackett

item.title: How to keep an ancient text interesting to students? Make it a summer blockbuster
item.description: English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.
item.content:encoded:
English teachers are excited to have a new adaptation of Homer's The Odyssey to use to get students excited about the epic poem.

(Image credit: Melinda Sue Gordon/Universal Pic/Melinda Sue Gordon)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: A town renamed its festival 'AI Love Irondale Day.' Then came the comments
item.description: Irondale, Alabama, rebranded its annual festival around AI, leading to online backlash that provoked a return to the original name.
item.content:encoded: Irondale, Alabama, rebranded its annual festival around AI, leading to online backlash that provoked a return to the original name.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Questions remain after fatal police shooting in Madison, Wisconsin
item.description: Police shot and killed a man who they say was stealing bicycles and checking car doors. The four officers involved have been placed on administrative leave.
item.content:encoded: Police shot and killed a man who they say was stealing bicycles and checking car doors. The four officers involved have been placed on administrative leave.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rizzo

item.title: A new threat in the Middle East sends oil over $100 a barrel again
item.description: The price of crude oil is rising again following Houthi claims of attacks on tankers in the Red Sea. What could it mean for gas prices, inflation and the global economy?
item.content:encoded: The price of crude oil is rising again following Houthi claims of attacks on tankers in the Red Sea. What could it mean for gas prices, inflation and the global economy?
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: The battle for control of Ukraine's military strategy
item.description: All Things Considered host Mary Louise Kelly speaks with NPR's Greg Myre and Charles Maynes about recent turmoil among Ukraine's military leaders.
item.content:encoded: All Things Considered host Mary Louise Kelly speaks with NPR's Greg Myre and Charles Maynes about recent turmoil among Ukraine's military leaders.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Over 4 million people are no longer on SNAP since last July
item.description: SNAP participation has been declining consistently and rapidly since last July, when President Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill into law.
item.content:encoded: SNAP participation has been declining consistently and rapidly since last July, when President Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill into law.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Families brace for separation as Haitians set to lose protected status next week
item.description: Many Haitians on Temporary Protected Status have had US-born kids. After a Supreme Court ruling stripped their status, they face possible deportation by month's end back to a still-dangerous Haiti.
item.content:encoded: Many Haitians on Temporary Protected Status have had US-born kids. After a Supreme Court ruling stripped their status, they face possible deportation by month's end back to a still-dangerous Haiti.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Along Lake Superior, erosion threatens the dead and the living's memories
item.description: Low lying cemeteries near shorelines face threats of erosion as water levels rise. That is especially true for two cemeteries near Lake Superior, the largest of the country's Great Lakes.
item.content:encoded: Low lying cemeteries near shorelines face threats of erosion as water levels rise. That is especially true for two cemeteries near Lake Superior, the largest of the country's Great Lakes.
item.dc:creator: Sam Fuqua
